title: 10 Proven Herbal Teas to Alleviate Colds
intro_desc: Herbal teas for colds are a natural remedy that helps alleviate symptoms associated with common colds by promoting relaxation, easing congestion, and boosting the immune system. The best herbal teas for colds include Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea purpurea, Zingiber officinale, Sambucus nigra, Thymus vulgaris, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Melissa officinalis, Rosmarinus officinalis, Echinacea angustifolia, and Lavandula angustifolia. If left untreated, colds can negatively impact daily life, causing fatigue, decreased productivity, and strained relationships due to prolonged illness and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tea is a popular remedy for colds because it has powerful decongestant properties. The tea contains a chemical called eucalyptol, which helps to break down mucus in the chest and sinuses. This makes it easier to breathe and can provide relief from congestion and coughing. When you drink the tea, the eucalyptol is absorbed into your body and helps to thin out the mucus, making it less sticky and easier to expel. This can help to reduce the severity of cold symptoms, such as a runny nose, sneezing, and a sore throat. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe a sore throat and reduce swelling in the sinuses. By drinking the tea, you may find that your cold symptoms start to clear up more quickly. The tea is also easy to make at home and can be enjoyed hot or cold, making it a convenient and accessible remedy for colds.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Catechins help fight colds by reducing inflammation and boosting the immune system, making it easier for the body to recover from illness.
Quercetin is good for colds because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off infections in the body.
Tannins are good for colds because they help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections by acting as antimicrobial agents that destroy the viruses and bacteria that cause illness.
Polyphenols are good for colds because they have powerful anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off the infection in the nose and throat.

remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for colds
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea angustifolia herbal tea to help with colds, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried Echinacea root or flowers, a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth, and a pot of boiling water. Begin by measuring out one to two teaspoons of the dried Echinacea, depending on your preference for strength. Place the Echinacea in the tea infuser or cheesecloth, and then carefully pour the boiling water over it. Allow the mixture to steep for five to seven minutes, or until the tea reaches your desired color and flavor. Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the Echinacea, and then add honey or lemon to taste. Once the tea has cooled slightly, you can drink it as a natural remedy to help boost your immune system and alleviate symptoms of a cold. Be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before consuming Echinacea angustifolia herbal tea, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or are taking medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is a natural remedy that can help with colds. It's made from the flowers and roots of the echinacea plant, which is native to North America. The tea has been used for a long time to boost the immune system and fight off infections. When you drink echinacea purpurea tea, the active ingredients, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, go into your body and start working. They help to reduce inflammation and fight off the germs that cause colds. The tea also has antiviral properties, which help to prevent the spread of the virus and reduce the severity of symptoms like a runny nose, cough, and sore throat. Drinking echinacea purpurea tea regularly may also help to prevent future colds from happening by keeping your immune system strong.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cichoric acid is good for colds because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the throat and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and recover from illness.
Alkylamides are good for colds because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat, making it easier to breathe and recover from illness.
Caffeic acid is good for colds because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off infections in the body.
Quercetin is good for colds because it has strong antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation in the body and fight off viruses that cause colds.
Tannins are good for colds because they have strong anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and fight off the viruses that cause colds.

remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for colds
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea angustifolia herbal tea to help with colds, start by gathering dried roots of the Echinacea angustifolia plant. If you don't have access to dried roots, you can purchase them from a health food store or online. Begin by boiling one quart of water in a large pot, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Next, add 1-2 teaspoons of the dried Echinacea roots to the pot, depending on your desired strength of tea. Let the roots steep in the hot water for 5-7 minutes, or until the liquid has reduced slightly and the flavors have melded together. Strain the tea into a large pitcher or container using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, and discard the solids. The resulting tea is a rich, earthy brew that can be enjoyed hot or iced, sweetened or unsweetened. Traditionally, Echinacea angustifolia tea is taken 2-3 times a day to help boost the immune system and combat cold and flu symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, is a popular remedy for colds. It's good for colds because it helps to reduce inflammation and ease congestion. The active compounds in ginger, like gingerols and shogaols, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe a sore throat and calm a cough. Ginger tea also has a natural expectorant effect, which means it can help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, ginger has antimicrobial properties that can help to fight off infections and promote healing. Drinking ginger tea can also help to warm the body and relieve a fever, which is often a symptom of a cold.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for colds because it helps reduce inflammation and fight off the virus that causes colds, thereby relieving symptoms such as sore throats and congested sinuses.
Gingerols are good for colds because they help to reduce inflammation and fight off the viruses that cause colds by stimulating the immune system and releasing chemicals that destroy the germs.
Shogaols are good for colds because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off infections in the body.
Shogaol is good for colds because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off the infection that causes cold symptoms.
Polyphenols are good for colds because they help boost the immune system, which can fight off viruses and reduce the severity of cold symptoms.

remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for colds
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea angustifolia herbal tea for colds, start by gathering dried roots of the plant, which can be found in health food stores or online. Rinse the dried roots gently with cold water to remove any impurities. Next, combine one teaspoon of the dried roots with one cup of boiling water in a teapot. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the liquid has turned a dark brown color. Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the roots. You can add honey or lemon to the tea for taste, but be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before consuming the tea, especially if you're pregnant, breastfeeding, or have any underlying health conditions. Once the tea has cooled, drink it 2-3 times a day to help boost your immune system and alleviate cold symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris tea, also known as thyme tea, is a natural remedy for colds. This is because it contains compounds that help fight off the viruses that cause colds. Thymol, a key ingredient in thyme, has antiviral properties that can help stop the spread of these viruses. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the swelling and congestion that often come with a cold. When you drink thyme tea, the thymol is absorbed into your body, where it can start to work its way through your system to help fight off the cold. Additionally, thyme tea is a natural expectorant, which means it can help loosen and clear mucus from your lungs, making it easier to breathe. This can be especially helpful when you're congested and feeling stuffed up.

Rosmarinic acid helps to fight colds because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nose and throat.

remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal teas for colds
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea angustifolia herbal tea, start by gathering dried roots of the plant, which can be found in health food stores or online. Begin by boiling one quart of water in a large pot, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Add one ounce of the dried roots to the water and let it steep for 10-15 minutes. You can also add other herbs like peppermint or ginger to enhance the flavor. Once the roots have steeped, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl or container. Discard the solids and let the tea cool. You can store the cooled tea in the refrigerator for up to three days. To make a single serving, pour one cup of the cooled tea into a mug and add honey or lemon to taste. Echinacea angustifolia tea is traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of the common cold, such as sore throats and congestion, and its antibacterial properties may also help to soothe a sore throat.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra tea, also known as elderflower tea, is a popular remedy for colds. The flowers of the elder tree contain flavonoids, which are powerful antioxidants that help fight off infections. These flavonoids also have anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nose and sinuses. When you drink Sambucus nigra tea, the flavonoids are absorbed into your body, where they can help to soothe a sore throat and reduce the severity of cold symptoms. The tea also has a natural expectorant effect, which helps to loosen mucus and make it easier to cough up. Additionally, Sambucus nigra tea has antiviral properties, which can help to combat the underlying viral infection that's causing your cold. By drinking Sambucus nigra tea regularly, you may be able to reduce the duration and severity of your cold symptoms.

Drinking this tea is believed to help boost the immune system and reduce the severity of cold symptoms. It's recommended to drink 2-3 cups of the tea a day to get the most benefits. However, before making or consuming any herbal remedy, it's best to consult with a healthcare professional, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tea is a popular remedy for colds. The leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help protect the body from infection and inflammation. When we have a cold, our airways can become swollen and congested, making it hard to breathe. Ginkgo biloba tea helps to open up these airways, making it easier to breathe. The tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease congestion. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba tea has antimicrobial properties that help fight off the underlying infection that causes the cold. This can help the body recover faster from a cold.

Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra tea has been shown to boost the immune system, helping your body to fight off the infection on its own. It also helps us feel more relaxed and calm, which can help our body recover faster from the cold. Additionally, the tea has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in our nose and sinuses, making it easier to breathe when we have a cold.

Echinacea also has antimicrobial properties, which help kill the cold virus and prevent it from spreading. It also supports the immune system, which helps the body fight off the infection faster. This helps to shorten the duration and severity of the cold, making it a useful tool for people who want to feel better quickly.

remedies.image_alt: Echinacea angustifolia herbal teas for colds
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea angustifolia herbal tea that helps with colds, you will need dried roots of the Echinacea plant, a teapot, and a strainer. First, take one to two teaspoons of dried Echinacea roots and put them in the teapot. Next, add one cup of boiling water to the teapot and let it steep for about 5-7 minutes. You can now drink the tea as is, or add honey or lemon to taste. It's recommended to drink this tea 2-3 times a day when you have a cold to help boost your immune system and reduce the severity of your symptoms.

supplementary_best: To remedy colds, a combination of Echinacea angustifolia, Echinacea purpurea, and Sambucus nigra is effective. Echinacea angustifolia helps boost the immune system and reduce inflammation, while Echinacea purpurea enhances its effects with its antimicrobial properties. Sambucus nigra's antiviral properties then target and eliminate the virus causing the cold. This combination works well together because Echinacea angustifolia's anti-inflammatory effects complement Sambucus nigra's antiviral properties, creating a powerful defense against the cold virus. The addition of Echinacea purpurea further enhances the immune-boosting effects of the combination, making it a strong remedy for colds.
supplementary_avoid: When you have a cold, some herbal teas can actually make things worse. For example, Yerba Mate tea can increase your heart rate and blood pressure, making congestion and headaches worse. Licorice Root tea can raise your blood pressure and worsen fluid retention, which can be a problem if you have a cold. Sage tea can dry out your mouth and throat, making it harder to recover from a cold. Peppermint tea can irritate your stomach, causing nausea and discomfort. Yarrow tea can cause stomach upset and interact with certain medications, making your cold symptoms worse. And finally, Juniper Berry tea can cause kidney damage in large amounts and can interact with certain medications. These teas can worsen your cold symptoms or interact with your medications, so it's best to avoid them while you're sick.
supplementary_other_remedies: When we're feeling sick with a cold, our bodies need extra help to fight off the infection. One way to get this extra help is by using natural remedies like honey. Honey has antibacterial properties that can help soothe a sore throat and reduce coughing. When we eat honey, it coats our throat and helps calm the irritation, making it easier to swallow. Some people also use raw, unfiltered honey to make a cough syrup by mixing it with warm water or lemon juice.  Another natural remedy is apple cider vinegar. Drinking apple cider vinegar can help loosen mucus in our chest, making it easier to cough up. This can help relieve congestion and make us feel better faster. Apple cider vinegar also has antibacterial properties, which can help kill off the germs that are causing our cold.  Some people also use garlic to help fight off colds. Garlic contains compounds that have antibacterial and antiviral properties, which can help kill off the germs that are causing our cold. When we eat garlic, it can help boost our immune system and make us less likely to get sick in the first place. However, eating raw garlic can be harsh on our stomach, so some people cook it or take it in supplement form.  Finally, some people use saltwater to help relieve congestion. When we breathe in warm, salty air, it can help loosen mucus in our chest and make it easier to cough up. This can help relieve congestion and make us feel better faster. Some people also use a neti pot to rinse out their nasal passages with a saline solution, which can help clear out mucus and debris that's clogging up their nose.
supplementary_related_ailments: When you have a cold, your body is fighting an infection caused by a virus. This can lead to symptoms like a runny nose, cough, sore throat, and fatigue. In addition to these common symptoms, people with colds may also experience headaches, congestion, and sinus pressure. These issues can be relieved with medicinal herbs like eucalyptus, peppermint, and ginger. Eucalyptus oil, for example, can help ease congestion by loosening mucus and opening up airways. This is because eucalyptus contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages. Peppermint oil, on the other hand, can help soothe a sore throat and ease headaches with its cooling and numbing effects. Ginger, a natural anti-inflammatory, can help reduce sinus pressure and alleviate pain. These herbs work by interacting with the body's natural processes to reduce inflammation and ease symptoms, providing relief from cold-related discomforts.
